name: MongoDB 7
version_status: CONFIRMED line / point release partly UNVERIFIED
what: Document database for flexible JSON-like data models, high-ingest workloads, and applications that expect MongoDB specifically.
solves: Schema-flexible application data, event capture, content documents, and vendor apps that are built around MongoDB.
users: Engineering teams and packaged applications that prefer document-oriented models or require MongoDB for supportability.
fit: Selective fit. Hylono’s primary site stack is more naturally PostgreSQL-centric, but MongoDB remains relevant because Novu and some app ecosystems expect it.
runtime: Single database server or replica set/sharded cluster with BSON document storage, indexes, background maintenance, and replication.
components: mongod, optional mongos/shards, persistent storage, backups, metrics, and client libraries.
deploy: Replica set for production durability; single node only for labs or low-risk internal services.
deps: Fast storage, memory headroom, backups, monitoring, and version-compatible drivers.
auth: SCRAM/X.509, RBAC, TLS, network controls, and app-scoped users.
apis: Mongo wire protocol, drivers, backup/restore tooling, metrics, and change streams in supported setups.
security: Treat as a production database: TLS, private networking, least-privilege users, and backup encryption.
setup_path: Use official 7.0 production docs. Prefer replica sets for any meaningful workload. Plan backups, monitoring, and index review from day one.
env: MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME, MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD, replica-set config, storage sizing, backup target credentials.
persistence: Snapshot plus oplog-aware backup strategy; test restores; watch index growth.
operations: Patch within the 7.0 line, review slow queries/indexes, monitor replication lag, and capacity-plan storage.
failure_points: Undersized storage, replica misconfiguration, index sprawl, and using MongoDB where relational guarantees were actually needed.
integration: Most relevant as a dependency for Novu or any app that is explicitly Mongo-first. It should not become the default datastore for the Hylono web platform.
verify: Confirm which apps truly require MongoDB and whether those should run in isolated clusters instead of sharing with unrelated workloads.
risk: Moderate. Operationally manageable, but it introduces another database family into the platform.
gdpr: Any personal data stored here requires the same EU residency, encryption, and retention discipline as PostgreSQL.
overlap: Partial overlap with PostgreSQL for general application storage, but not a replacement for Mongo-dependent apps.
codex_read_first: Read the app-specific dependency notes before assuming MongoDB belongs in a new design.
codex_best_path: Use MongoDB only for apps that explicitly depend on it; avoid inventing new Hylono domain models on MongoDB unless there is a concrete reason.
codex_never_assume: Never assume MongoDB should be the default because it is already running. Verify supportability and data-model trade-offs first.

sources.why: Primary release-note source for MongoDB 7 line.
Used to confirm current release family researched.
